targets.expr: (sum(rate({"traces.span.metrics.calls", "@resource.service.name"="$workload", "k8s.pod.name"=~"$pod", "status.code"="STATUS_CODE_ERROR"}[5m])) by ("k8s.pod.name") or sum({"traces.span.metrics.calls", "@resource.service.name"="$workload", "k8s.pod.name"=~"$pod"} * 0) by ("k8s.pod.name")) / sum(rate({"traces.span.metrics.calls", "@resource.service.name"="$workload", "k8s.pod.name"=~"$pod"}[5m])) by ("k8s.pod.name")

targets.expression:
fields @timestamp, `resource.attributes.k8s.pod.name as pod, body
| filter `resource.attributes.k8s.workload.name = "$workload"
| filter `resource.attributes.k8s.pod.name like /(?i)$pod/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 200

panels.title: Traces by Pod (select a pod above)

panels.description: Percentage of keyspace lookups that were cache hits.
panels.title: Cache Hit Rate (%) — $workload

panels.description: Throughput broken down by Redis command type.
panels.title: Commands / sec — $workload

panels.description: A session is active when it's either running on CPU or waiting for a resource to become available so that it can proceed (e.g. IOPS or CPU). For optimal performances, you should not have more AAS than the total number of vCPU. Investigate AAS in RDS performance insights. See also https://www.kylehailey.com/post/setting-the-record-straight-a-comprehensive-guide-to-understanding-the-aas-metric-in-databases and https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/USER_PerfInsights.Overview.ActiveSessions.html

targets.expr:
max(
    max(rds_instance_vcpu_average) by (instance_class)
    * on (instance_class)
    group_left(aws_account_id,aws_region,dbidentifier)
    max(rds_instance_info{dbidentifier="eks-petclinic-pythoninstance1"}) by (aws_account_id, aws_region, dbidentifier, instance_class)
)

panels.title: RDS Database Insights Average Active Sessions
